location.office_time_required: occasional work at an Airbnb office or attendance at offsites, as agreed to with your manager

seniority.level: Senior IC / Lead (likely Staff or Principal equivalent)
level_signals: Reports to Director, Programs and Business Operations
Presents to senior leaders in weekly/monthly/quarterly business reviews
Comp range $168K-$203K base consistent with senior IC at Airbnb

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Qualified candidates will ideally have a minimum of 10+ years of work experience, preferably with combined exposure to data-modeling, business analytics, and project management in a dynamic entrepreneurial environment.
A proven bias to action and ability to own a project from conception through to post-mortem.
Ability to work in a world of uncertainty and ambiguity - to create clarity from chaos.
A world class written and verbal communicator with the ability to present at all levels of an organization in a succinct, clear and eloquent manner.
A proven track record of being able to storytell, influence and rally diverse cross-functional stakeholders against a common mission or goal using both qualitative and quantitative insights.
Ability to not only dive into details to drive execution but also zoom out to contextualize specific recommendations/initiatives within the broader company strategy.
Ability to multi-task by prioritizing work and coordinating required support across various functions to achieve project goals and objectives.
Impeccable organizational skills, attention to detail and ability to document processes & decisions made.
Experience implementing and scaling initiatives across globally dispersed teams.
Experience working cross-functionally with multiple departments.
Comfort with manipulating large datasets in excel is a must.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: A familiarity with AI, AI data analysis and AI tools would be considered an asset.

scope.surface: Americas regional business operations and growth programs within the Global Markets / Chief Business Officer organization
scope.business_model: marketplace (B2C and B2B), global travel and hospitality platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Americas regional business objectives (supply, demand, regulatory)
Key initiative progress tracked in weekly/monthly/quarterly business reviews
Host and guest community growth metrics
core_responsibilities: Align all functions to achieve Americas's regional business objectives in partnership with the Director, Regional Business Operations and Growth
Own the conception, execution, and measurement of cross-functional initiatives end-to-end
Drive regular business operation meetings and initiate meaningful conversations - leading weekly meetings with cross-functional project teams
Use all available data sources and tools to analyze macro and micro trends and present analysis, insights, and actionable plans to leadership

Assist the Regional Director and Regional BizOps Director in owning the performance narrative for key metrics and deliver the story to senior leaders in weekly, monthly, and quarterly business reviews

compensation.equity_form: eligible for bonus, equity, benefits, and Employee Travel Credits

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($168K-$203K base) plus equity and travel credits
Clear reporting line (Director, Programs and Business Operations)
Specific scope — Americas regional BizOps within a named org (CBO / Global Markets)
Explicit cross-functional partners named (field ops, marketing, product, legal)
red_flags: Must live in a US state where Airbnb has a registered entity — excluded states list is dynamic and not provided in-line
SQL listed as 'strongly preferred' but Excel is 'a must' — classification as must-have vs. nice-to-have is ambiguous for SQL
10+ years minimum experience is a high bar and the JD uses 'ideally' as a qualifier, creating slight ambiguity about hardness
No mention of visa sponsorship; work auth listed as unclear

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, years required, and reporting level)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed)
location.work_auth (inferred as no-sponsorship from US state residency requirement language, but not explicitly stated)
ambiguities: SQL is described as 'strongly preferred' but given the data-heavy nature of the role and Excel being listed as 'a must' in the same sentence, SQL was classified as hard severity; could reasonably be soft
The years_experience requirement uses 'ideally have a minimum of 10+' — 'ideally' softens the language but the role context suggests this is a true floor; classified as hard
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; work_auth set to no-sponsorship as inference from US state residency requirement, visa_sponsorship set to unclear
